compiled_state.title: Implement resolve_scalar_trailers in apply-attribution-trailers.sh
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description: Implement resolve_scalar_trailers([task-id]) in plugins/dso/scripts/apply-attribution-trailers.sh. Uses PATH-resolved .claude/scripts/dso ticket show to traverse parent chain for DSO-Task, DSO-Story, DSO-Epic, Jira-Ticket. Reads $ARTIFACTS_DIR/reviewer-findings.json for DSO-Review-Score. Emits --trailer 'Key: value' per non-empty scalar. Test isolation: tests prepend a mock bin/ dir to PATH containing a dso script that echoes fixture ticket JSON (use printf to create the mock, chmod +x). AC: (1) task-id with parent story + grandparent epic (mocked via PATH) -> DSO-Task/Story/Epic flags; (2) reviewer-findings.json with score -> DSO-Review-Score flag; (3) no context -> empty output; (4) all T4-test tests pass; (5) validate.sh --ci passes.

comments.body:
Gap Analysis AC amendment: resolve_scalar_trailers must handle dso ticket show failures gracefully.

In worktree sub-agent contexts or CI environments, .tickets-tracker/ may not be mounted. A hard failure in resolve_scalar_trailers would violate the non-blocking contract.

Amended implementation: If 'dso ticket show' fails (exit non-zero or empty output at any point in the parent chain traversal), emit no scalar trailers for that missing level and continue. Never exit non-zero due to ticket lookup failure.

Additional test case to add to T4-test:
- test_exits_0_and_omits_scalar_trailers_when_ticket_show_fails: Given DSO_BINARY mock returns non-zero for dso ticket show, when resolve_scalar_trailers is called with a task-id, then exit code is 0 and no DSO-Task trailer is emitted (graceful degradation, not a blocking error).
comments.author: Test
